qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded HTTP request body accumulation in HTTP Stream transport enables denial-of-service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function and root cause as the advisory: src/transports/http/server.ts readRequestBody accumulates POST body chunks with body += chunk.toString() and never enforces the maxMessageSize configuration used by the HTTP Stream transport.

selected_finding.title: Unbounded HTTP request body accumulation in HTTP Stream transport enables denial-of-service

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause in readRequestBody. A copied PoC was executed under .judge against mcp-framework 0.2.21; after harness-only fixes, it showed the HTTP Stream server accepting and parsing an oversized /mcp POST body instead of enforcing maxMessageSize, followed by server process termination and verified denial of service.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-353c-v8x9-v7c3 describes src/transports/http/server.ts readRequestBody lines 224-240 accumulating request body chunks without a size limit while maxMessageSize is configured but unused.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_0a25d0cd/unbounded-http-stream-body-dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies the same readRequestBody body += chunk.toString() loop and dead maxMessageSize configuration.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_0a25d0cd/.judge/poc_runs/unbounded-http-stream-body-dos/run.log shows mcp-framework 0.2.21 HTTP Stream listening on /mcp, /health returning {"ok":true}, an 8 MB body exceeding the 4 MB limit being processed with HTTP_CODE:500 rather than size rejection, and the server process terminating with the verifier reporting DoS success.

poc_executions.observed_effect: Initial copied PoC failed before reaching the vulnerable logic: the script requested tag v0.2.21, fell back to main, and npm install failed.

After correcting the copied PoC to checkout mcp-framework-v0.2.21, the run still failed before vulnerable-code activation because the target checkout was mounted read-only while npm install tried to write into it.
After making the temporary target checkout writable, the run built mcp-framework 0.2.21 but the minimal test server exited before binding HTTP because it had no tools capability.
After registering a minimal tool in the copied test server, startup still failed before HTTP handling because the generated test project imported zod without declaring it.

The copied PoC started mcp-framework 0.2.21 on HTTP Stream, verified /health returned {"ok":true}, sent an 8 MB POST body to /mcp despite a configured 4 MB maxMessageSize, observed JSON parsing rather than a size-limit rejection, and then observed the server process terminate with the verifier reporting denial of service.
